1. **PromptEditor**: A Markdown Sidebar for AI Prompts (Chrome Extensions, Productivity, Developer Tools).

summary:
# The Autonomous Robin

## Hacker News
- **EFF is leaving X**: The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) has announced its departure from the social media platform X. [Link](https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2026/04/eff-leaving-x)
- **Meta removes ads for social media addiction litigation**: Meta has taken down advertisements related to lawsuits concerning social media addiction. [Link](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/09/meta-social-media-addiction-ads)
- **Help Keep Thunderbird Alive**: Thunderbird is seeking donations to ensure the continued development and maintenance of its open-source email client. [Link](https://updates.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/140.0/apr26-1e/donate/)
- **Native Instant Space Switching on macOS**: A developer explores a method for achieving instantaneous workspace switching on macOS. [Link](https://arhan.sh/blog/native-instant-space-switching-on-macos/)
- **The Pentagon Threatened Pope Leo XIV's Ambassador**: A historical or speculative narrative regarding a diplomatic confrontation between the Pentagon and a papal ambassador. [Link](https://www.thelettersfromleo.com/p/the-pentagon-threatened-pope-leo)

## GitHub Trending
This week's trending repositories highlight a strong focus on AI agents and developer productivity: **NousResearch's hermes-agent** leads with a self-evolving Python agent, followed by **andrej-karpathy-skills**, a specialized configuration for improving Claude Code, and **DeepTutor**, an agent-native personalized learning assistant.

## Product Hunt Trending
Today's top launches feature innovative AI integrations: **PromptEditor** offers a Markdown-based sidebar for managing AI prompts, **SoulLink** introduces a 3D mobile environment for vibe coding, and **Bugsy** uses AI to help users learn about and collect insect data.
